howItWorks.intro: This paycheck calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Net Pay = Gross - Federal Tax - State Tax - FICA
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How is take-home pay calculated?
faq.answer: Gross pay minus federal income tax, state income tax, and FICA (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45% = 7.65%). This does not include benefits deductions.

faq.question: What is the effective tax rate?
faq.answer: Your effective rate is lower than your marginal bracket because only income within each bracket is taxed at that rate. A $65K salary has an effective federal rate around 13-16%.

faq.question: What about pre-tax deductions?
faq.answer: 401(k) contributions, HSA, and health insurance premiums reduce your taxable income. Add these for a more accurate estimate.

faq.question: How often should I get paid?
faq.answer: Common schedules: weekly (52), biweekly (26), semi-monthly (24), monthly (12). Biweekly is most common in the US.

faq.question: Is the Paycheck Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Paycheck Calculator?
sections.body: The Paycheck Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your annual salary, pay periods/year, effective fed tax rate, you get instant results including gross per paycheck, federal tax, state tax. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting gross per paycheck right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your annual salary and need to find the right gross per paycheck. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Annual Salary ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your annual salary — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: pay periods/year, effective fed tax rate, state tax rate. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your gross per paycheck, federal tax, state tax. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your annual salary before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both gross per paycheck and federal tax — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your state tax rate, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
